title: HIGH: tool output not scrubbed for secrets before LLM/session storage
description: Secret redaction (sandbox.rs is_sensitive_env_name/value) is applied only to subprocess env (input). Tool RESULTS flow verbatim to the LLM (via cap_oversized_tool_results) and to session storage. cat .env / echo $API_KEY / env leak. Fix: a single redact_secrets() boundary on tool output reusing the existing detector. sandbox.rs:121,181; ui/events.rs:317 (ANSI-only).

title: HIGH: /allow remove <n> doesn't revoke engine grant
description: remove_session_allowlist_at only mutates the display list; the engine allowlist (runtime source of truth read by SessionAllowlistPolicy) keeps the grant. Revoked perms stay active. Must remove engine entry by matched (op, original), not index.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #204 (TDD, 4 new tests); 2104 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Perm review F1: bash arg-side path checks for file-mutating commands
description:
SECURITY GAP found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Today (post-M3, fbcc09b) dirge's bash permission flow extracts ONLY redirect targets (>, >>, &>, etc.) via extract_redirect_targets and routes them through write rules. Arguments of file-mutating commands (rm, cp, mv, chmod, chown, ln, mkdir, rmdir, touch, tee, dd) are NOT extracted — they go only through the bash command-pattern rules. 

Concrete bypass: a user who configures bash rules permissively (e.g., 'rm *: allow' for convenience) silently allows 'rm /etc/passwd' even though write rules deny /etc/**. Opencode (shell.ts:374-410) walks the 'command' AST nodes, identifies file-mutating heads, and routes each positional path arg through the external_directory / write permission.

Port: extend src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs with an extract_mutation_paths(command) function that walks the tree-sitter 'command' nodes; for each command whose head matches the list above, extract positional args that look like paths (skip -flags / --long-flags) and emit them. In src/agent/tools/bash.rs check_bash_segments, after the existing redirect-target loop, walk extracted mutation paths and route through enforce(tool='write', Scope::PathResolve(path)).

title: Perm M3: port maki's tree-sitter bash analyzer (close git&&rm bypass)
description:
SECURITY: 'git diff && rm -rf /' currently allowed because dirge's bash redirect-target check (src/agent/tools/bash.rs:350) routes through bash rules with the file path as input, which has no path-style match → falls to default Allow. Pre-existing pre-fix-for-7403792.

Solution: port maki's tree-sitter bash analyzer verbatim from /Users/yogthos/src/maki/maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:33-43 (parser thread_local), 394-439 (collect_commands walker), 441-475 (analyze_bash + complexity gates). The walker splits compounds via 'pipeline'/'list' AST traversal and extracts every 'command' / 'redirected_statement' / 'subshell' / etc node; each segment then goes through the permission chokepoint independently.

Behavior at completion:
- 'git diff && rm -rf /' → enforce('bash', 'git diff') + enforce('bash', 'rm -rf /') — second check fires
- Subshells / command substitution mark whole command 'complex' → forces prompt (conservative)
- Pipes split into separate segments
- Quoted operators correctly NOT split (AST respects quoting)

Depends on: dirge-{M1}

Maki license is GPL-compatible — verify before copying. Add 'Ported from maki-agent/src/permissions.rs' attribution comment.

title: Loop: orphaned tool_call_id (partial storm suppression / interrupted batch) 400s the next request
description: When the storm breaker suppresses SOME tool calls in a multi-call assistant message (partial), or a batch is cancelled/interrupted mid-way, fewer tool-result messages are appended than there were tool_calls. The assistant message keeps all tool_calls, so the next provider request has an unanswered tool_call_id and 400s ('must be followed by tool messages responding to each tool_call_id'). Recoverable harness bug. Fix: backfill a synthetic error tool-result for every unanswered id so history stays well-formed and the model sees the gap.

title: Permission: allow-always never matches multi-line bash commands (regex not DOTALL)
description: Root cause of persistent re-prompts. Command-style permission patterns (Pattern::new_command, path_style=false) compile to a regex WITHOUT the DOTALL flag, so '.*' cannot span newlines. A session grant like 'npx *' -> '^npx(?: .*)?$' fails to match 'npx tsx -e "import x\nimport y"' (multi-line -e script), so the command re-prompts forever even after the user picks allow-always. Models very commonly emit multi-line -e/-c scripts and heredoc-ish args, so this breaks allow-always broadly. Fix: build command-style regexes with RegexBuilder::dot_matches_new_line(true); keep path patterns line-sensitive. Add a deterministic test corpus of realistic compound/multi-line commands verifying the suggest->allow->recheck invariant.
close_reason: 3 root causes fixed (DOTALL command regex, bare-command ** optional args, benign-builtin default-allow alignment) + npx/node/python/python3 removed from defaults (arbitrary code) + deterministic gating corpus. Merged in PR #271.

title: Background-injected MCP tools invisible under dynamic_tool_search
description: Regression from PR #266 (background MCP loading). When dynamic_tool_search is enabled, build_loop_tools snapshots the ToolSearchTool registry + an empty loaded-set at BUILD time, before MCP connects. extend_loop_tools appends MCP tools to loop_tools but updates neither, so filter_tool_defs (rig_stream_factory.rs) filters them out of every request AND tool_search can't discover them — the model can neither see nor call background-loaded MCP tools. Fix: extend_loop_tools must also mark injected tool names into the shared tool_def_filter loaded-set so they pass the request filter. dynamic_tool_search is opt-in so default users are unaffected.
close_reason: extend_loop_tools now marks injected MCP tool names into the tool_def_filter loaded-set so they pass the request filter under dynamic_tool_search. Merged in PR #267.

title: harness/lsp: startup deadlock if plugin calls harness/lsp at load time
description: LSP responder spawns after plugin load; a top-level (harness/lsp) call at load blocks the worker on reply_rx forever (no drainer yet), and main can't reach the spawn. Fix: spawn responder before plugin-load loop.

title: Questionnaire custom answer: text runs off to the side instead of soft-wrapping
description: When typing a custom answer in the question UI, text scrolls off horizontally and disappears. Need soft-wrap onto new lines and to keep the cursor/end-of-text always visible.
close_reason: Merged in PR #216: questionnaire custom answer soft-wraps via wrap_editor

title: Input textbox: doesn't scroll past max height; typed text vanishes; no up/down arrow nav
description: The TUI compose input box stops expanding at a max height and further typed lines aren't rendered (text disappears). Need: vertical scroll so the newest lines stay visible, and Up/Down arrow keys to move the cursor across the wrapped/entered lines.
notes: Scroll half fixed in PR (input-scroll-and-nav). Arrow up/down soft-wrap nav split to a follow-up issue (needs display/raw cursor mapping).

title: LLM connect/send errors ('error sending request') not retried
description: classify_error returns Other for reqwest connect/send failures like 'Http client error: error sending request for url (...)', so the retrying_stream_fn wrapper does not retry them. Add 'error sending request' (and related connect-failure shapes) to the Network branch so transient connection failures get the standard backoff retry.

title: MED-HIGH: retry loop scaffolding re-implemented 3x; btw/summarize ignore cancellation
description: recovery.rs classification is shared, but the attempt/loop/backoff-sleep driver is hand-copied 3x. Only agent_loop/retry.rs honors AbortSignal during backoff and emits StreamEvent::Retry; provider/mod.rs:593 (btw_query) and provider/summarize.rs:141 sleep uninterruptibly and retry silently. Fix: policy.run_with_retry(fn, signal) helper owning the cancellable loop + retry callback.
notes: Duplication half fixed + merged in PR #209 (run_with_retry helper). RESIDUAL: cancellation-during-backoff needs an AbortSignal threaded through btw_query/summarize call sites — still open.
close_reason: Duplication fixed in PR #209 (run_with_retry). Cancellation-during-backoff residual is narrow: only /btw is affected (the task tool already drop-cancels btw_query via select! racing), and threading an AbortSignal through the signal-less call sites isn't worth the API churn for that one path. Triage-closed.

title: MED-HIGH: lsp anchors relative paths to self.cwd before perm check
description: lsp.rs joins relative paths to self.cwd before check_perm_path_resolve, while read/write/edit/apply_patch reject relative and let the engine resolve against working_dir. self.cwd can drift from engine working_dir, so the path checked != path used. Fix: route through shared resolve_absolute / pass raw like other FS tools. lsp.rs:226 vs read.rs:184/write.rs:94/edit.rs:137.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #208: lsp rejects relative paths via shared guard; cwd anchor removed

title: HIGH: UI re-hardcodes divergent path-tool list vs engine is_path_tool_name
description: UI hardcodes a smaller path-tool set (read|write|edit|list_dir) in 3 files; the engine canonical is_path_tool_name has ~14 incl semantic tools. grep/glob/apply_patch/find_files/lsp/repo_overview/semantic mislabeled in UI. Fix: UI calls engine::is_path_tool_name. tool_display.rs:35, permission_ui.rs:29, ui/mod.rs:2555 vs engine/classify.rs:36. Part of chokepoint-A.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #206: storm classifiers derive from tool_operation; permission_ui path arms deduped

title: HIGH: storm-breaker uses hardcoded mutating/exempt tool lists vs metadata
description: Doom-loop storm-breaker partitions tools via its own default_mutating/default_exempt name lists (comment admits 'kept in sync with BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES'). A new mutating tool gets execution_mode=Sequential (dispatches right) but is absent from default_mutating, so the loop guard goes blind to it. Fix: consult tool.execution_mode()/metadata. storm.rs:216,230 vs builder.rs:743,1581,1625. Part of chokepoint-A (tool metadata registry).
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #206: storm classifiers derive from tool_operation; permission_ui path arms deduped

title: Hard cutover: op-based permission config schema
description: Replace legacy per-tool PermissionConfig fields (write/bash/edit + ToolPerm enum + tools map) with the op-based rules: Vec<RuleConfig> array. No legacy translation path. Migrate all test sites + docs (CONFIG.md, docs/permissions.md).
close_reason: Merged in PR #203 — op-based rules schema replaces legacy per-tool config; all 2100 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 2.5b: scavenge from ContentBlock::Text, not just Thinking
description: Reasonix scavenges from BOTH reasoning AND content channels (loop.ts:910-913, repair/index.ts:71). Dirge only scans ContentBlock::Thinking (run.rs:558-566). A model emitting DSML invoke blocks in regular text content (the common R1-in-content case) is silently missed. Extend the filter_map to include Text blocks. End-to-end test: DSML orphan call in Text-only response gets recovered.

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 2.5a: hoist truncation pass above storm filter
description: Reasonix runs truncation BEFORE storm filter (repair/index.ts:88-109 then 113-121). Dirge runs truncation inside validate_and_repair at dispatch time — AFTER storm filter. Result: two calls whose truncated args repair to the same form survive storm and dispatch identically. Move repair_truncated_json invocation to run.rs between scavenge merge and storm.filter_calls; remove the pre-pass from validate_and_repair. End-to-end test: two truncated-to-same calls → storm suppresses one.

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 2: scavenge provider-coverage test matrix
description: Scavenge only reads ContentBlock::Thinking today. DeepSeek's reasoning_content field, OpenAI o1 reasoning, Anthropic extended-thinking may not all route through rig into Thinking. Add provider matrix tests asserting each surface produces a Thinking block that scavenge_tool_calls can extract. Fix any adapter gaps found.
close_reason: Completed + merged as PR #178 (scavenge provider-coverage matrix); status was stale in_progress.

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 1: truncation brace-closer in tool_input_repair
description: Port Reasonix's repair/truncation.ts brace/bracket/string-stack closer to dirge tool_input_repair.rs as RepairKind::TruncationFixed. Currently a model hitting max_tokens mid-tool-call has no recovery; the call fails as 'invalid' in RepairStats. Reasonix tracks an open-stack of {/[/" chars while parsing left-to-right and emits the matching closers when the stream ends unbalanced.

title: --loop mode termination skips on_session_end
description: HeadlessLoopExit::MaxIterations at main.rs:901 returns Ok(()) without firing the hook. Third top-level exit path (interactive + --print + --loop); only first two wired.

title: Session::reset_to_new + plugin_tree paths skip both lifecycle hooks
description: reset_to_new at src/session/mod.rs:766 rotates session.id + clears messages with no hook fires. Only caller is plugin_tree.rs:101 (TreeOp::NewSession). Plugin-driven session transitions are a complete blind spot.

title: /quit + Interrupted exit paths skip on_session_end
description: cmd_quit returns Err(Interrupted); ui/mod.rs:1471 matches and breaks the event loop. The dirge-bx4g hook at line 893 only catches idle Ctrl+C/D, not error-driven exits. /quit silently misses every on_session_end.

title: auto-compaction in agent_loop bypasses on_pre_compress
description: run_compaction_pass_with_focus in src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:170 calls compression::build_summary_prompt directly without touching memory_provider. The on_pre_compress hook only fires for the manual /compress slash command. Auto-fold is the common case; plugin providers' insights get dropped silently.

title: fix: session_search returns current session's own turns
description: builder.rs:338,749 pass current_session_id=None to SessionSearchTool::new. Dedup in extras/session_search.rs:169 requires the id to be set. Thread session id through. TDD.

title: fix: skill action='view' typo in builder preamble
description: builder.rs:207 tells the model to call skill with action='view'; real enum is load/create/edit/patch/delete/list. Calls fail. Change to 'load'. TDD.

title: fix: memory tool action names in system prompt
description: prompt.rs:87 advertises actions view/write/delete; real schema at memory.rs:76 is view/add/replace/remove. Model following the prompt errors out on writes/deletes. Fix prompt to match tool. TDD.

title: Phase 8 integration: memory injection into system prompt panics on file read errors — agent builder uses unwrap() for load_memory/load_pitfalls. Disk error or corrupt file crashes the entire agent startup.
close_reason: false alarm — code uses if let Ok(...) pattern, not unwrap(). Reviewer misread the code.

title: Phase 4 background_review: passes only 'response.to_string()' as transcript — tool calls and their results are missing from the review context. The review model cannot assess tool interaction quality.
close_reason: Fixed: review now receives full session transcript via build_transcript(session) which includes user messages, assistant text, tool call names+args, and tool results. 5 new tests verify the transcript builder handles all message types, tool states (completed/interrupted/failed), truncation of large results, and system messages.

title: ratatui migration: integration + delete old paint paths
description: Phase 6: switch the main loop to terminal.draw(|f| renderer.render(f)) on every state change. Delete the old draw_panel, draw_left_panel, draw_left_panel_idle, draw_avatar, draw_bottom, render_viewport, ensure_room direct-stdout paths. write_line / write just push to chat buffer.
acceptance_criteria: Main loop calls terminal.draw on each redraw; no direct stdout writes remain in renderer.rs; UI matches prior visual goals.

title: ratatui migration: bottom strip widget (avatar + input + overlay)
description: Phase 5: avatar box, input box, overlay all rendered through one BottomStrip widget. Single overlay slot (set_alert_overlay/clear). When overlay active, input is replaced inside same frame.
acceptance_criteria: BottomStrip widget renders avatar centered vertically + input editor or overlay; verticals align with chat frame ║.

title: ratatui migration: side panel widgets
description: Phase 4: left panel (AGENT STATUS / SUBAGENTS) + right panel (SYSTEM with sub-panels) become ratatui widgets. Sub-panels (SYSTEM LOAD / MCP / LSP / TODOS / MODIFIED) are light-rounded Block widgets with left-aligned content.
acceptance_criteria: LeftPanel + RightPanel widgets render; sub-panels stack vertically; content left-aligned per user spec.

title: ratatui migration: top/bottom frame widget
description: Phase 2: implement the unified top frame as a ratatui widget that paints ═══[AGENT STATUS]═══╔═══[AGENT LOG STREAM]═══╗═══[SYSTEM]═══ into a Buffer. TDD with TestBackend asserting buffer cells.
acceptance_criteria: TopFrame widget renders the unified frame into a Buffer; tests verify exact cell content at row 0 + chat bottom frame row.

title: ratatui migration: Layout + Rect model
description: Phase 1 of UI ratatui migration. Add ratatui dep, define a Layout struct that computes named Rects (chat, left_panel, right_panel, top_frame, chat_bot_frame, bottom_strip, avatar_box, input_box, right_margin, status) from (cols, rows, input_rows, overlay_active). TDD: test rect tiling (no overlap, full coverage), test edge cases (narrow terminal, very tall input).
acceptance_criteria: ratatui dep added; Layout::new(cols, rows, ...) returns named Rects; unit tests cover tiling correctness for several terminal sizes.
close_reason: Layout struct + 9 TDD tests landed in 9eb3bcf

title: Empty Read chambers re-manifesting on parallel reads
description:
User reports: agent does 7 parallel Read calls. First 6 chambers show TOP + BOTTOM only (no content). LAST chamber has full content. Same symptom shape as dirge-jzj (closed in commit 70e7290) but appears to be regressing or has a separate cause.

Reproduction: parent agent doing 'Let me look at the remaining files' while 4 background subagents are running (at the cap). Reads in parent fire in parallel (agent_loop default is Parallel). ToolResults arrive out-of-order; fresh-chamber preamble at ui/mod.rs:2597 should paint a new chamber + content for each non-current id but isn't.

Possible regressions:
- Phase D-E (subagent_chat_rx handler) added new mpsc::select arm. If event ordering interacts badly with parallel ToolResult handling, chambers could be racing.
- write_line_to_chat for inactive chats pushes to slot.buffer + bumps slot.lines. Could affect content_row() computation if called between a parent's chamber-open and content-paint? But the active chat is always 0; slot writes go to non-active. Shouldn't matter.
- Auto-resume gate (dirge-9xo) is gated on !is_running — shouldn't fire during parent tool execution.

Needs: live RUST_LOG trace of LoopEvent::ToolExecutionStart / End for one such sequence to see WHICH ids land in WHICH state at the preamble check. Static analysis maxed out.
notes: Diagnostic instrumentation landed in commit 311f208. Reproduce with RUST_LOG=dirge::ui::chamber=trace; the per-event trace stream will show whether the body is missing on arrival (tool-side bug) or being dropped by the chamber routing (UI-side bug).
close_reason: Cannot reproduce after extensive investigation. 15 isolated tests + 4 parallel layer audits (bridge, tokio::select!, LoopTool dispatch, renderer) all clear. The dirge-jzj fix logic is intact in the current code path (verified via git blame). Diagnostic tracing remains in place under 'dirge::ui::chamber=trace' if the symptom ever reappears in production. See ba35f6a + this commit for the full investigation trail.

title: Subagent: main agent doesn't auto-resume when background subagent completes
description:
Currently when a background subagent (task tool with background=true) finishes, BackgroundStore::notify queues a system-reminder. The parent agent only sees it on the NEXT user turn via prepend_pending_notifications. So the parent sits idle until the user prompts.

Opencode's approach (packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts:215-240, resumeWhenIdle + continueIfIdle): when a background subagent completes, opencode injects a synthetic user message and starts a new turn on the parent — auto-resuming the loop.

Fix for dirge: in the UI loop's subagent_chat_rx handler (Complete/Failed events), if the parent is currently idle, automatically spawn a new turn with the pending-notification text. The mechanism is already there (BackgroundStore has notifications, prepend_pending_notifications composes the prompt) — just trigger it instead of waiting for user input.

title: UI: allow safe slash commands while agent is running
description:
Today every keypress with an agent active hits 'agent is busy — wait, interrupt (Ctrl+C), or use /quit' (src/ui/mod.rs:2013). But read-only commands (/tasks, /help, /sessions list, /tree show) don't actually need the agent to be idle. They just inspect state. Maki and opencode let inspection commands run during agent activity.

Fix: maintain an allowlist of safe-during-agent commands. The dispatch path checks the allowlist BEFORE the busy gate — anything in the list runs immediately; everything else still gates on is_running.

Allowlist (read-only / inspection):
- /tasks (lists chat windows)
- /help
- /sessions (no-arg: list)
- /tree (no-arg: show)
- /memory list
- /skill list
- /prompt (no-arg: show active)
- /model (no-arg: show active)

Commands that MUTATE state (/clear, /compress, /cd, /prompt set, /model switch) stay gated.

title: UI: ! / !! bash escape from input prompt (port from maki)
description:
Typing '!<cmd>' at the input prompt should execute the shell command and feed the output back to the agent. '!!<cmd>' executes WITHOUT telling the agent (silent — purely for the user's local terminal use).

Port verbatim: maki-ui/src/app/shell.rs:27-50 parse_shell_prefix() — self-contained, no ratatui coupling. Returns ShellPrefix { prefix_len, command, visible }.

Integration: in dirge's submission path (src/ui/mod.rs, where Enter triggers send-to-agent), detect '!' / '!!' prefix BEFORE forwarding to agent. Spawn the shell via tokio::process; on completion: if visible=true, inject as a user-visible message that flows to the agent; if visible=false, print to scrollback and skip the agent.
close_reason: Already implemented in src/shell.rs:1+ (ShellPrefix::Visible/Invisible) and wired at src/ui/mod.rs:1706/1737. Initial audit missed it because the module lives at src/shell.rs not src/ui/shell.rs.

title: UI: fuzzy search Ctrl-F via nucleo_matcher (port from maki)
description:
Current Ctrl-F search (src/ui/mod.rs:1369-1410, update_search at :4937) uses substring match via line.to_lowercase().contains(&query_lower). Maki uses nucleo_matcher for fuzzy matching ranked by score.

Port: copy from maki-ui/src/components/search_modal.rs:
- lines 8-9 (nucleo imports)
- lines 24-29 (SearchMatch struct)
- lines 157-185 (update_matches: nucleo::Atom::indices, sort by score descending)
- lines 305-351 (match-building from buffer lines)

Keep dirge's existing search overlay rendering (draw_search_bar) and key handling. Just swap the matching algorithm. Add nucleo-matcher to Cargo.toml.

title: UI: raw-mode bare LF in renderer.write produces staircase on reasoning stream
description:
USER REPORT: reasoning text (DarkMagenta) renders with each word on a new line, progressively indented — classic raw-mode \n-without-\r staircase pattern. Pre-existing bug (predates today's work).

The renderer has two writeln!(stdout, ...) call sites that emit bare LF:
- src/ui/renderer.rs:741 — write_line: writeln!(stdout, "{}", chunk) — chunk + \n
- src/ui/renderer.rs:809 — write (streaming inline): bare writeln!(stdout) — \n only

In raw mode (enable_raw_mode disables OPOST/ONLCR), \n is JUST LF: cursor moves down one row but column stays. Each subsequent call DOES MoveTo before writing, which should reset the col — yet user reports staircase. Likely cause: a buffering / order-of-ops interaction where the MoveTo escape sequence lands AFTER the LF reaches the terminal, or somewhere the MoveTo is skipped on a corner case.

Defensive fix: replace both writeln! sites with explicit write!(stdout, "\r\n") (or chunk + \r\n). Belt-and-suspenders: even if MoveTo handles it correctly normally, the explicit CR resets col immediately so any later code that bypasses MoveTo also stays sane.

User context: iTerm2, no tmux. Reasoning text only — content tokens render fine (they use replace_from + render_viewport which paints per-row with explicit MoveTo).

title: Perm review F2: alias write/apply_patch to edit permission
description:
HIGH-correctness gap found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Opencode (permission/index.ts:291-301) defines EDIT_TOOLS = ['edit', 'write', 'apply_patch'] and aliases all three to the same permission name 'edit' during rule evaluation. A user writing 'edit: deny' blocks all three uniformly.

Dirge keeps them as separate permission tools (one rule namespace each). A user writing 'permission: { edit: { **: deny } }' expecting to lock down all edits still has write and apply_patch silently going through (post-M4 they Ask, but a follow-up 'write: allow' from the user would unintentionally re-open the gate).

Port: in enforce() at src/agent/tools/mod.rs, when the tool name is 'write' or 'apply_patch', ALSO consult the 'edit' rules. Take the more restrictive result of the two checks (any deny wins, any explicit ask beats allow). Document the aliasing in PermissionConfig docs.

title: Perm M4: flip unmatched-tool default from Allow to Ask
description:
Today src/permission/checker.rs:103 has default_action = Action::Allow. Anything not explicitly configured falls through to Allow without prompting — a security gap especially for write/edit/apply_patch which currently have NO default rules installed at all (mismatch with bash which has default_bash_rules, mcp_tool which defaults Ask since 27bd70a).

Flip default to Ask. Add builtin-allow list for safe read-only tools (port maki's BUILTIN_ALLOW_RULES at permissions.rs:16-24, adapted for dirge's tool set: read/glob/grep/list_dir/list_symbols/find_definition/find_callers/find_callees/get_symbol_body/repo_overview).

write/edit/apply_patch/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill/memory all become Ask by default unless explicitly allowlisted. Document the migration in README + CHANGELOG. Users with existing configs unaffected — only the no-config baseline changes.

Add --yolo CLI flag (currently only via config). Maps to set the global allow_all atomic (port maki's PermissionManager::toggle_yolo at permissions.rs:219-222).

Depends on: dirge-{M2}, dirge-{M3}

title: Perm M1: single chokepoint (port maki's enforce shape)
description:
Refactor dirge's 3 permission entry points (check_perm, check_perm_path, check_perm_path_resolve in src/agent/tools/mod.rs) into a single chokepoint patterned after maki's PermissionManager::enforce (maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:283). One function, takes (tool, scope), routes internally based on tool category. No behavior change for users — pure refactor with all existing callers updated. Behind-the-scenes the new fn calls the same PermissionChecker logic.

Ref: maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:283-350 — port the signature shape (async, returns Result<(), PermissionError>, takes &EventSender + user_response_rx + cancel for UI integration). Adapt to dirge's existing AskSender + PermCheck.

title: Perm M2: unified rule schema (port maki's TOML shape via JSON)
description:
Replace dirge's per-tool field PermissionConfig (separate Option<ToolPerm> per built-in tool) with maki's uniform shape: { tool_name: { allow: [patterns], deny: [patterns] } }. Single PermissionRule struct (maki-config/src/lib.rs:268-273). Keep existing JSON config (dirge uses JSON not TOML), but flatten the schema. Add a dual-read path so old config.json files (existing user configs in the wild) still parse — log a deprecation warning and auto-migrate on save. Migration test required.

Depends on: dirge-{M1}

title: UI: per-tool-call-id chamber state (parallel tool execution)
description: The UI tracks chamber state via a single bool tool_chamber_open + Option<String> last_tool_name. Under parallel tool execution (the default per agent_loop/types.rs:402), multiple ToolExecutionStart events fire before any ToolExecutionEnd. Subsequent ToolCalls close prior chambers prematurely (now via passive close after 7403792, previously with false 'denied' wording). ToolResults that arrive after a newer ToolCall's chamber opens land as out-of-place '↳ trailers' below the wrong chamber. Fix: chamber state keyed by tool_call_id (HashMap), each in-flight tool gets its own chamber frame, the abort/passive close functions take an id.

title: H-batch1-2: history up/down byte-offset panic risk on multi-byte input
description: ui/input.rs:988-1023 — col = self.cursor - line_start is byte distance; self.cursor = (pos + col).min(target_line_end) can land mid-codepoint when previous line has multi-byte chars. Subsequent replace_range/slice panics. Convert column to char index then back to byte offset on target line.

title: H-batch1-1: bash inherits all env vars including API keys
description: tools/bash.rs:208-320 no .env_clear(). Sensitive vars (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY, PARALLEL_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.) flow to every bash child. Use .env_clear() + curated allowlist (PATH, HOME, USER, LANG, TERM, etc.). See pi bash-executor.ts.

title: C8: /cd, /worktree, /wt-exit, /prompt, /regen-prompts silently drop LSP
description: ui/slash.rs:1290, 1300, 1763 + analogues in mod.rs — every build_agent call after these commands passes None for lsp_manager. User silently loses LSP for the rest of the session after first /cd. Re-thread lsp_manager (and re-root for /cd/worktree). opencode cd handler re-roots LSP.

title: C7: serialize_conversation drops tool_calls entirely
description: provider.rs:418-429 emits only [role]: content; msg.tool_calls (args, results, errors) invisible to summarizer. Tool-heavy sessions lose bulk of state post-compact. Include structured tool I/O like pi's compaction prompt.

title: C6: compaction summary truncates input to 6000 chars
description: provider.rs:312-320 cuts the prefix to first 6KB then appends '... [truncated]'. A 300K-token session is summarized from ~1500 tokens of content. Feed the full prefix (opencode session/compaction.ts:136-294) or chunk-summarize (pi compaction/). Pair fix with C7.

title: C5: ! cmd injects shell output into LLM context unfenced
C4: bash redirection target not permission-checked
description: tools/bash.rs:322-339 and semantic-bash never inspect redirect targets. echo pwned > /etc/something matches the safe echo ** rule and writes to /etc/something. Extract redirect/path args per command and route through path gate. See opencode shell.ts:191-208 pathArgs.

title: C3: compound_statement bypass in semantic-bash splitter
description: semantic/adapters/bash.rs:162-175 treats compound_statement/if_statement/while_statement/for_statement/case_statement/function_definition as opaque single segments. { rm -rf /tmp/foo; } matches no per-command rule and falls through to Allow. is_complex (line 127) doesn't flag these either. Mirror opencode shell.ts:127 using descendantsOfType('command') — recurse into every command descendant.

title: C2: webfetch SSRF via HTTP redirects
description: tools/webfetch.rs:270-273 builds reqwest client without redirect::Policy::none() or custom policy. validate_url_host_safety runs once on initial URL; 30x to RFC1918/169.254 is silently followed. Install custom redirect::Policy::custom re-running validate_url_host_safety on each Attempt::url().

title: C1: apply_patch uses unresolved path after perm check (symlink-swap risk)
description: tools/apply_patch.rs:251 calls check_perm_path (not check_perm_path_resolve), then apply_create/update/delete/rename operate on raw user-supplied path. Other tools (read.rs:161, write.rs:88, edit.rs:133) route through resolved_path. H12 fix didn't propagate. Thread check_perm_path_resolve per op and pass resolved string into apply_*.

title: H1: propagate AbortSignal into JanetLoopTool::execute
description: JanetLoopTool::execute takes _signal and drops it. Plugin tool handlers run unboundedly inside spawn_blocking holding the PM mutex; the user's Ctrl+C/Esc has no effect until the handler returns. Phase-6 wired AbortSignal into native tools; plugin tools must too.\n\nFix: race the spawn_blocking join against wait_for_cancel(signal) (same pattern as the phase-6 wrapper in builder.rs). On cancel return Err("cancelled"). Document that Janet is single-threaded so handlers can't be preempted — only joined. Plugin authors should keep work bounded.

title: C1/C2: fix customType wrapper + add e2e bridge test for renderer dispatch
description: Phase 9d renderer lookup uses wrong JSON nesting level — payload.get('type') against the {role,content} wrapper always returns None, so registered renderers never fire end-to-end. Smoke test calls invoke_message_renderer directly with a hand-built payload so it passes a broken path.\n\nFix: extend harness/add-custom-message to (customType content &opt display); wrap as {role:'custom', customType, content, display:true} matching pi's CustomMessage shape (messages.ts:48). UI consumer reads customType at top level. Add bridge/UI-equivalent test that walks the full LoopMessage::Custom -> AgentEvent::CustomMessage -> renderer-resolve path so future drift is caught.

title: Path traversal via .. survives lexical-fallback when both canonicalize calls fail
description: permission/checker.rs:495-513 — when canonicalize(joined) AND canonicalize(parent) both fail (nonexistent subdir + ..), falls through to lexical joined.to_string_lossy(). Path::starts_with matches components, so /cwd/nonexistent/../../etc/passwd classifies as internal. Need lexical .. normalization in fallback.

title: Provider SSE stream has no per-chunk timeout
description: agent/runner.rs:259 stream.next().await with no tokio::time::timeout — stalled provider hangs agent forever. opencode chunk-timeout pattern: per-chunk read deadline.

title: ANSI injection in permission ALERT prompt
description: ask_req.tool / ask_req.input rendered un-sanitized at mod.rs:2584-2585. Reopen path already sanitizes — asymmetric. Sec impl: ANSI at the permission-decision moment.

title: Chat history ignores 120-col content_width cap
description: max_line_width and wrap_line use raw content_cols, so on wide terminals scrollback overflows the centered band into divider/panel margin.

title: R1: fix 3 critical plugin bugs (FFI panic, dialog deadlock, init hang)
description: From the plugin subsystem audit: (1) wrap JanetCFunctions in catch_unwind so Rust panics don't unwind across the C-FFI boundary into Janet; (2) cancel send_dialog's reply_rx.recv() on worker shutdown so the worker thread doesn't block forever when the UI exits mid-dialog; (3) add timeout to the init handshake so a worker panic before init_tx.send() doesn't hang the main thread. Also: (4) bounds-assert wrap_string's i32 cast for the unlikely >2GB case, (5) make take_string_slot atomic to close the race window, (6) don't eat unrelated user events in the dialog arm.

title: Loop: max_turns kills active human steering; reset budget on fresh user steering
description: The per-run turn counter (max_turns, default 100) accumulates across the whole run and is never reset when the user interjects/steers. A user actively steering a progressing agent gets killed by the cap as if it were a runaway loop. Runaway loops are caught by the storm breaker; the cap is a cost ceiling for AUTONOMOUS runs. Fix: reset turns_taken when a fresh USER steering message is consumed (not file reminders or plugin/critic follow-ups).

title: Memory: compact (evict oldest) instead of failing the write when budget is full
description: When a memory add() would exceed the per-file char budget, it returned 'Char budget exceeded' and the model gave up. Instead, compact: evict the oldest entries until the new one fits. Only a single entry larger than the whole budget is a real error (split it). Surface the compacted count in the success message.

title: Add approval_provider: LLM auto-approves permission prompts
description: Optional config approval_provider. When set, a permission Ask is routed to that LLM (with a clear safety prompt explaining dangerous ops: deleting files, committing/modifying outside project or /tmp, network code-exec, sudo) instead of prompting the human; the LLM returns ALLOW/DENY and the system acts on it. Mirror critic_provider wiring. Document in docs/permissions.md.
close_reason: approval_provider added: per-session evaluator on the checker; only intercepts Ask; fail-safe to human; shared one-shot LLM helper. Merged in PR.

title: Make background-injected MCP tools search-gated under dynamic_tool_search
description: Follow-up to dirge-ffwa. The shortcut fix marks injected MCP tools as always-loaded (always shipped in the request) which erodes dynamic_tool_search's token savings for heavy-MCP users. Proper fix: make ToolSearchTool.registry an Arc<Mutex<Vec<ToolMeta>>>, store the handle on AnyAgent, and have extend_loop_tools append the injected tools' meta to it (discoverable via tool_search, hidden until requested) instead of marking them loaded. Keeps behavior identical to build-time MCP tools.
close_reason: ToolSearchTool registry is now Arc<Mutex<Vec<ToolMeta>>>; extend_loop_tools appends injected MCP tools' meta to it so they're search-gated (discoverable via tool_search, not always-shipped). Merged in PR #268.

title: Startup: load MCP tools in background so the TUI draws immediately
description: Even with parallel connect_all, the interactive UI still blocks on MCP before the first frame because the slowest server (e.g. 'npx -y chiasmus' cold start) gates build_agent's collect_tools. Build the interactive agent with NO MCP tools, draw the UI immediately, and connect+collect MCP in a background task; deliver (manager, wrapped tools) over a channel to run_interactive's select loop, which injects the tools into the live agent (next clone picks them up) and stores the manager so the panel lights up. Non-interactive (--print/--loop) keep connecting synchronously.
close_reason: Interactive TUI now builds the agent without MCP tools and draws immediately; a background task connects+collects+injects MCP tools into the live agent and lights up the panel when ready. --print/--loop unchanged. Merged in PR #266.

title: Startup: parallelize MCP connect_all to cut time-to-first-frame
description: connect_all (src/extras/mcp/mod.rs:32) connects MCP servers sequentially in a for-loop, so startup pays the SUM of every server's spin-up. With several servers each doing an 'npx -y <pkg>' cold start (seconds) and a 10s per-server init timeout, the UI takes several seconds to appear. Parallelize the connects with futures::join_all so startup waits only for the slowest server, not all in series. Keep bookkeeping (insert/log order, failure handling) deterministic.
close_reason: MCP servers now connect concurrently (futures::join_all); startup waits for slowest server, not the sum. Merged in PR #265.

title: Right panel: MODIFIED box tall when empty + bash file mutations don't propagate
description: Two right-side-panel bugs. (A) The MODIFIED sub-panel takes all remaining vertical space even when empty, rendering very tall; it should be one line tall like TODOS '(none)' and grow with content. (B) Files created/deleted via bash (heredoc 'cat > f', 'rm', 'mv') do not appear in MODIFIED because bash.rs never calls mark_modified; only write/edit/apply_patch do. Reuse extract_redirect_targets + extract_mutation_paths (already computed for permission checks) to mark bash mutations.
close_reason: Fixed: empty MODIFIED collapses to 1 line; bash heredoc/rm/mv mutations now propagate. Merged in PR #264.

title: Generalize syntax-error feedback: missing-token + per-language delimiter localization
description: Make pre-write syntax errors mechanically useful for all tree-sitter languages: (1) name the expected token from tree-sitter MISSING nodes; (2) generalize the Lisp delimiter-balance scanner to brace languages via per-language lexing rules.

title: Lisp paren feedback + retry budget + additive LSP extensions
description: (1) Enrich tree-sitter pre-write errors for Lisp with a delimiter-balance summary so the model stops counting parens. (2) Increase retry budget for transient LLM API failures. (3) Additive lsp extensions config (extend builtin list).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #261 (paren feedback, retry budget, lsp extend_extensions)

title: UI/permission tweaks: whitespace, autocomplete, double-click, compound-bash perms
description: Batch of minor fixes: (1) trim/collapse whitespace in slash command args; (2) ghost-text slash autocomplete with right-key accept; (3) double-click word select; (4) compound bash command re-prompts because allow-rule covers only the first segment.

title: F6.3: bounded in-loop LLM critic (tier 3)
description: Optional critic_provider role; when configured, run one bounded LLM critique at finalization on substantive runs. CriticFn callback mirrors SummarizeFn; reuses summarize_with_model. Off unless critic_provider set.

title: F6.2: outcome-aware verifier (tier 2)
description: Upgrade VerifierGate to track whether a build/test command ran and PASSED or FAILED (via bash exit-code marker). At finalization: red build → fix-it nudge; edited-but-no-verification → verify nudge; verified-pass → silent. Records post-execution at finalize_executed_tool_call.

title: Phase D: cross-feature integration tests + consolidated docs
description: Prove all 6 guidance features compose: base-prompt cohesion (F2+F3+F5) + combined loop test (F1+F6 in one run). Consolidated docs section.

title: F6: pre-finalization verifier gate
description: Cheap signal-based in-loop critic: nudge to verify before done when code edited but no shell command ran. Reuses get_followup_messages finalization seam + FileTouchTracker pattern. Bounded once per run, no LLM call.

title: F4: in-session reflexion memory
description: Accumulate abandoned approaches across a run and re-surface them in the repeat-loop guard. Reuses storm detection + reflect-then-pivot injection. Reflexion (Shinn 2023) episodic buffer.

title: F5: uncertainty-calibrated ask-vs-assume
description: Base-prompt calibration for when to ask the user vs proceed: weigh cost/recoverability/inferability/divergence; otherwise proceed with the most reasonable interpretation and state the assumption. Self-assessed (no logprobs available). Research: Ask or Assume?

title: F3: progress / tool-preamble narration
description: All-models base-prompt guidance: brief up-front plan for multi-step tasks + terse progress notes between major steps. Scoped to long tool runs, NOT the final reply (reconciled with the terse-output ethos). Research: GPT-5.1 tool preambles.

title: F2: definition-of-done + pre-reply self-check + stop conditions
description: All-models base-prompt guidance: a single fast self-check before claiming done, an explicit definition of done, and stop conditions (when to stop vs hand back). Research: GPT-5 prompting guide.

title: F1: few-shot tool-use exemplars via lexical (nucleo) retrieval
description: Curated exemplar corpus; query-aware retrieval by token overlap using nucleo-matcher (reuse in-repo dev-dep, promote to dep). Inject top-k exemplars for the user's task into the turn context. All models.

title: Epic: close harness-guidance gaps (few-shot, self-check, progress, reflexion, ask-calibration, critic)
description: 6 general-purpose model-agnostic guidance features from the research gap analysis. TDD, branch-per-feature, sequential merge, reuse existing infra, integration tests at the end.

title: P4: structured tool-error self-repair hint
description: On malformed/failed tool call return structured repair hint so model self-corrects. Wire into tool-result seam.
close_reason: Already implemented: tool_input_repair.rs (validate_and_repair + format_structured_error wired into dispatch). No new work needed.

title: P3: dual-encode load-bearing rules into tool descriptions
description: Fold read-before-edit, no-repeat-failed-call, parallel-vs-sequential into tool descriptions at the action boundary. Helps all models.

title: P2: DeepSeek-gated guidance markdown fragment
description: prompts/steering/deepseek.md embedded via include_str!, appended to preamble only when vendor==Deepseek. Structural framing, plan-execute-verify, success/never contract, anti-repetition.

title: P1: model-family resolver (chat vs reasoner, deepseek vs other)
description: Pure resolve_family(provider, model_id) -> ModelFamily{vendor,kind,supports_system_prompt,supports_tools}. Wire into build_agent_inner.

title: Epic: DeepSeek-aware harness steering for better reasoning
description: Add model-family detection + DeepSeek-gated guidance + cross-model tool-description hardening + structured tool-error self-repair. Baked-in/automatic, no config keys. Research-backed: structural prompting, plan-execute-verify, anti tool-call drift/repetition.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #250. P1-P3 implemented + tested; P4 (structured tool-error self-repair) already existed in tool_input_repair.rs. Live A/B + unit tests green (2222).

title: nrepl plugin: fix try-body compile error, bencode framing, eval timeout; correct docs
description: The bundled nrepl plugin never loaded (multi-form try bodies are a Janet compile error). Also: bencode reader dropped coalesced/partial TCP messages; os/time timeout math was 1000x off; README wrongly required external janet on PATH.

title: Hidden side panels don't give freed space to chat
description: The /display feature hides side panels but Layout::new always reserves symmetric gutters regardless of show_left_panel/show_right_panel. Hidden panels leave blank reserved space instead of expanding the main chat pane. Fix: thread panel visibility into layout so a hidden panel's gutter is reclaimed by the chat band.
close_reason: Layout is now visibility-aware via Layout::with_panels; hidden panel gutters are reclaimed by the chat band

title: /display command + config to choose visible TUI panes
description: Add /display left|main|right slash command and 'display' config key to control which side panels show, independently per side. Main pane always shown.

title: harness/lsp: (harness/lsp?) reports available when LSP runtime-disabled
description: Predicate checks compile-time symbol existence, not runtime manager. When lsp_manager is None (config-disabled), predicate is true but queries return nil -> plugin json/decode crashes. Make availability reflect a live bridge.

title: Input box: Up/Down arrow nav across soft-wrapped display rows
description: After the scroll fix (dirge-5w9v), Up/Down still move by HARD newlines only (prev_line_start/next_line_start); in a wrapped paragraph with no \n, Up falls through to history recall. Make vertical motion wrap-aware: map the cursor to its display (row,col) via wrap_editor's projection and move to the adjacent display row, then back to a raw byte offset (handling the editor's paste-marker display projection). Reuse wrap_editor; don't duplicate wrap logic.

title: MED: cancelled tool future keeps running detached (work leak)
description: Dispatcher races wait_for_cancel vs tool future; on cancel returns an aborted result but lets the tool future continue (tools.rs:515-529 comment confirms no force-kill). In-process work (MCP call) leaks. bash converts abort->process kill; MCP/LSP rely on Drop. 3 cancellation primitives (AbortSignal atomic, CancellationToken, killpg). Fix: standardize one token + race-then-drop/kill helper. tools.rs:515-543, tool.rs:41.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #210: Notify-backed AbortSignal (instant cancel) + drop-contract regression test; verified drop-on-cancel is sound

title: MED: data/config dir overrides honored inconsistently; web-enable precedence duplicated
description: DIRGE_DATA_DIR honored in storage.rs:15 but ignored by loop transcript.rs:5 (uses dirs::data_dir directly) -> split state. DIRGE_CONFIG_DIR honored in storage.rs:23 but plugins(main.rs:574)/themes/prompts re-derive ~/.config/dirge. Also webfetch/websearch enable precedence (cfg||env_true) copy-pasted builder.rs:457 & :905. Fix: export storage::dirs_path/config_path as the single base; add Cli::resolve_web*_enabled.
close_reason: Merged in PR #212: exa_api_key/web-enable helpers, DIRGE_DATA_DIR transcript + DIRGE_CONFIG_DIR plugin routing

title: MED: no tool-result boundary pipeline (truncation x3, no shared cap)
description: LLM cap (~12000 chars, compression.rs) and UI cap (500/tool_result_max_chars, tool_display.rs:251) are separate codepaths w/ different markers; plus 5 per-tool caps (bash 256KB, background 3000 silent, grep 4096, review 2000, list_dir 200). A bash result truncates up to 3x w/ 3 markers; config knobs only bind UI. Fix: one TruncationPolicy / boundary layer (redact->cap->format). Pairs with dirge-tkyn.
close_reason: Merged in PR #214: shared head_cap (output byte caps, fixes silent drop) + required_nonblank (per-action arg checks)

title: MED: conditional required-for-action validation hand-rolled per multiplexer tool
description: memory/skill/lsp re-derive which fields are required for the chosen action with bespoke ok_or/is_none + divergent messages, no repair. JSON Schema can't express conditional-required. Fix: dirge-hints conditional_required rule ({when:{action:add},require:[content]}) in validate_and_repair. memory.rs:129, skill.rs:131, lsp.rs:197.
close_reason: Merged in PR #214: shared head_cap (output byte caps, fixes silent drop) + required_nonblank (per-action arg checks)

title: MED: absolute-path enforcement duplicated across 4 tools despite semantic tag
description: read/write/edit/apply_patch each re-implement Path::is_absolute() with divergent error strings, though a semantic:'absolute_path' dirge-hint tag (tool_input_repair.rs:710) is designed to own it (only read declares it; only used for md-link unwrap). Fix: validate_and_repair rejects AbsolutePath-tagged fields failing is_absolute, one message. read.rs:184/write.rs:94/edit.rs:137/apply_patch.rs:276. Part of chokepoint-A.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #207: shared require_absolute_path helper

title: MEDIUM: op:* config rules use path-style globbing
description: pattern_for_op maps OpSpec::Any to path-style Pattern::new, so blanket {op:*} rules with a single * silently under-match commands/mcp keys (git * misses git push origin/main).
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #204 (TDD, 4 new tests); 2104 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Central MemoryProvider hook dispatcher (audit finding A)
description: The 4 MemoryProvider hooks (on_memory_write, on_session_end, on_pre_compress, on_session_switch) fire from 40 callsites across 11 production files: main.rs, ui/mod.rs, ui/plugin_tree.rs, ui/slash/cmd_session.rs, ui/slash/mod.rs, provider/mod.rs, agent/review.rs, agent/tools/memory.rs, agent/agent_loop/run.rs, agent/agent_loop/integration.rs, extras/memory_provider.rs. Adding a 5th hook requires hunting every site; easy to forget one → silent inconsistency. Build a central dispatcher (single 'fire_hook' helper or a HookBus) so every callsite goes through one path. Foundation for future hooks (after_turn, post_review, etc).

title: Memory curator + lifecycle (audit finding B)
description: MEMORY.md and PITFALLS.md have no consolidation, no stale-detection, no audit. Skills get the full ops surface (curator, 30d/90d lifecycle, .usage.json telemetry, pinning, .curator_reports/, LLM consolidation via dirge-odv3). Memory entries get none of it — MEMORY.md grows forever with no path to merge overlapping facts or retire stale ones. Build a memory curator that mirrors src/extras/skills/curator.rs for memory entries: mechanical lifecycle pass + LLM consolidation pass. Reuse the review.rs runner infrastructure where it fits.

title: Auto-compaction / Phase 3: turn-end per-tool-result cap
description: Reasonix shrinks every tool result >3000 tokens at every turn-end (TURN_END_RESULT_CAP_TOKENS). Dirge only shrinks via prune_tool_outputs INSIDE a fold pass triggered at 75% context — a single 50KB tool output stays verbatim until fold fires. Add per-result cap at every turn boundary, independent of fold.

title: more descriptive session summaries in /sessions listing
description: Current /sessions preview is the last 30 chars of the last message — useless for tests/empty sessions ('...stale', '...outgoing'). Derive a better preview: (1) compaction summary's Active Task / Goal line if present, (2) first user message truncated, (3) fall back to last message.

title: CI matrix: build each feature group at least once
description: Default cargo build doesn't compile acp/plugin/lsp/loop/semantic-* code paths. Recent regressions slipped through: ACP build broke when build_agent gained session_id, plugin_hooks_tests broke when run_agent_loop gained memory_provider. Add CI matrix exercising each feature group.

title: on_session_switch fires in inconsistent state window
description: ui/mod.rs:2241 fires after DB rotation + messages drain, but BEFORE save_session (line 2245). A provider that queries DB or on-disk JSON during the hook sees inconsistent triple state. Move the hook to after save_session OR move save_session to before the rotation.

title: compaction handler doesn't rebuild agent — SessionSearchTool stale
description: ui/mod.rs:2236 fires on_session_switch + updates session.id but does NOT rebuild agent. The agent's SessionSearchTool was constructed with the pre-rotation session_id; after rotation it excludes the wrong session. dirge-502b fixed this for /session swap but not for compaction rotation.

title: --print save_session? short-circuits past on_session_end fire
description: main.rs:816 uses ? which propagates save errors up, skipping the on_session_end fire at line 823. Failed save is exactly when plugin providers need the hook (their backend is the only durable record). Fix: fire before save OR handle save error inline.

title: /session swap fires on_session_end but never on_session_switch
description: cmd_session.rs:105 rotates session.id via *ctx.session = s after firing on_session_end. The incoming id never triggers on_session_switch — exactly the case the trait docstring describes ('cache per-session state, document ids, counters').

title: fire on_session_end on clean CLI exit
description: No call site fires on_session_end when the user exits the program normally (loop terminates). Add a shutdown hook in run_interactive or main.rs after the loop exits cleanly.

title: fire on_session_end on compaction-driven session rotation
description: ui/mod.rs:2224 changes session.id after compaction — the OLD session has effectively ended. Fire on_session_end with the pre-rotation transcript before the id assignment so plugin providers receive the session boundary.
notes: On reflection this is on_session_switch (mid-process id change), not on_session_end. Add the hook to the trait and fire it at the compaction rotation point.

title: fire on_session_end on /clear (session reset)
description: cmd_clear truncates session messages without firing on_session_end. /clear is a genuine session-reset boundary; hermes fires the hook on /reset analog. Wire it before the clear.

title: use MemoryProvider trait through builder.rs preamble
description: builder.rs:212 still calls the concrete MemoryToolStore::format_for_system_prompt. A plugin provider's prompt block won't appear. Change builder.rs to hold Option<Arc<dyn MemoryProvider>> and call through the trait.

title: subagent session_id propagation
description: Subagents spawn via task tool. If they get session_id=None, their session_search includes the parent's transcript + their own. Audit task spawn path and decide policy: inherit parent (excludes parent's transcript) vs distinct id vs explicit None for fresh subagents. Add test.
close_reason: not-a-bug, invariant pinned with regression test

title: wire MemoryProvider::on_session_end + on_pre_compress hooks
description: PR 157 added the lifecycle hooks but no call site invokes them. Plugin providers that override on_session_end (end-of-session fact extraction) or on_pre_compress (preserve insights through compaction) get no signal. Invoke provider.on_session_end at the same point as spawn_background_review in done.rs, and provider.on_pre_compress in the compression path.

title: add: MEMORY_GUIDANCE and SESSION_SEARCH_GUIDANCE to system prompt
description: Hermes injects tool-gated guidance at session start (system_prompt.py:105-186) telling the model when to persist and when to search past sessions. Dirge has none in prompt.rs. Add equivalents.

title: add: in-session skill creation/patching guidance to system prompt
description: Model only learns about skill create/patch from post-session review. Hermes injects SKILLS_GUIDANCE telling model to save skills after complex success and patch outdated skills mid-task. Add equivalent to prompt.rs:88.

title: background subagent completion: main agent not notified
description: When a background subagent completes, the main agent doesn't see the result until the user prompts again. The completion event should surface into the parent's context as a system reminder OR the parent should poll task_status. Investigate the LifecycleReceiver flow and whether completions inject a follow-up message.

title: subagent output truncation: 'The subagent's output was truncated'
description: Task tool returns 'The subagent's output was truncated' for non-trivial subagent runs. The truncation point is too aggressive — full subagent output should be returned to the parent (or at least configurable). Check the task tool's response assembly + any inline char/line caps.

title: subagent UI: stream output in subagent tab + kill command
description: Subagent chats currently show only '<you> prompt' and '(subagent running…)' — the assistant's streaming tokens and tool calls aren't surfaced in the tab. Users have no way to see what a subagent is doing OR to abort one. Stream the subagent's AgentEvents into its chat slot, and add a /kill <id> (or Ctrl+K on focused tab) to abort.

title: permission: accept-mode prompts for in-cwd writes; same-folder re-prompts
description: Accept mode is supposed to auto-approve work inside the cwd. Users report (a) edit/write on files in cwd still pops the permission dialog, and (b) when 'Allow always' is selected for a folder, subsequent writes to the same folder re-prompt instead of being remembered. Investigate the rule eval + session allowlist persistence.

title: memory tool: add without prompting
description: The memory tool currently prompts for confirmation on add. Should auto-approve memory adds (low-risk, scoped to ~/.dirge/memories), or accept the user's standard/accept/yolo mode without re-prompting on top.

title: spawn_review_runner ignores review_provider config role
description: src/provider/mod.rs:1228 builds the review-runner's stream_fn from self (the main agent's client + model). The Config refactor in 42e778f added ConfigRole::Review and the review_provider key, but spawn_review_runner doesn't consult it — so setting review_provider in config.json silently has no effect on which model runs background review. Fix: when ConfigRole::Review resolves to a different provider than ConfigRole::Default, build_agent should construct a review-specific stream_fn (mirroring the Phase 4 escalation pattern) and stash it on AnyAgent for spawn_review_runner to pick up. When equal, fall back to the current self-built stream_fn (no duplicate client construction). Surfaced during audit of dirge-7ls (cache isolation fix landed correctly, but exposed this independent drift).

title: Phase 4 background_review: fires on EVERY Done event with no rate limiting or cost gating — could cause massive LLM API costs for users on paid plans with frequent session completions
close_reason: Fixed: review now rate-limited to 15-minute intervals via AtomicU64.

title: Phase 8 integration: last_user_prompt tracking misses several input paths — shell bang command path, loop iteration path, plugin followup path, and interjection queue drain path do NOT update last_user_prompt, causing stale prompts in session DB
close_reason: Fixed: last_user_prompt now updated at all 10 spawn_runner call sites: main text submission, shell bang command, worktree merge, loop iteration start, loop iteration continue, plugin followup, both interjection drain paths (Done + Interjected), ContextOverflow respawn, and question response.

title: Phase 6 curator: CuratorState atomic_write_sync may fail if .archive/ parent dir doesn't exist — atomic_write_sync writes to a temp file in the same directory and renames, but doesn't create missing parent dirs
close_reason: Already handled — CuratorState::save() calls create_dir_all(parent) before atomic_write_sync, and archive_skill() calls create_dir_all(archive_dir) before rename. No missing parent directory issue.

title: Phase 4 background_review: review fork shares the same ToolCache as main agent. Review writes to memory/skills can pollute the main agent's tool cache.

Phase 5 session_search: FTS5 query input is not sanitized — user-provided search terms are passed directly to FTS5 MATCH without escaping special FTS5 syntax chars (*, AND, OR, NOT, NEAR, parentheses, quotes)
close_reason: Fixed: added sanitize_fts5_query() function in session_search.rs that wraps queries in double quotes and strips FTS5 special characters (*, ", (, )). 5 new tests verify normal queries pass through, special chars are stripped, quotes are removed, empty-after-clean returns empty, and whitespace is trimmed.

title: Phase 2 memory_store: locking performed INSIDE reload_and_detect_drift + add/replace/remove — but reload opens the file BEFORE acquiring the lock. TOCTOU exists between file existence check and lock acquisition. FileLock uses create_new not flock — two processes could both pass the exists check before either creates the lock file.
close_reason: Already fixed — the lock (acquire_lock) is called before duplicate/budget checks in add(), before substring match in replace(), and before substring match in remove(). Drift detection (reload_and_detect_drift) runs under the lock. No TOCTOU window.

title: find_git_root doesn't canonicalize paths — symlinks in the path chain can cause incorrect root detection
close_reason: Fixed: find_git_root now calls cwd.canonicalize() before walking up, resolving symlinks in the path chain.

title: FileLock uses create_new which leaves stale lock files on crash — no deadlock detection or PID-based staleness check
close_reason: Fixed: FileLock now writes PID into lock file on creation. On contention, is_lock_stale() reads the PID and checks if the process still exists via kill(pid, 0) on Unix. Stale lock files from crashed processes are detected and cleared automatically on first contention check.

title: find_git_root doesn't handle git worktrees (.git as file)
close_reason: Fixed: find_git_root now checks for .git file with gitdir: prefix (worktree marker) in addition to .git directory. is_git_root_marker() helper handles both cases.

title: run_print max_turns wired to agent_loop LoopConfig
plugin hook contract tests (currently zero)
agent_loop integration tests (zero outside h7_smoke)
App-level mouse selection working through modal UI
description: Implement char-granular drag-to-select with auto-copy. Must work through alerts, dialogs, pickers, permission prompts. Renderer scaffolding (selection_active/start/end, buffer_pos_at, selected_text, copy_to_clipboard) already exists from previous incarnation; this wires events, adds ratatui REVERSED highlighting, and adds a cross-cutting selection::handle() helper consumed by every user_rx.recv() site so modal loops can't block it.

title: UI: subagent overview in left panel
description:
The left panel area (between col 0 and the chat content_indent) is mostly empty space today, used only for the bottom-row avatar (5 chars wide). Reclaim it as a real-time subagent overview.

Content per subagent:
- short task id
- status (Running / Completed / Failed)
- truncated prompt
- elapsed time

Source: BackgroundStore::list() + subagent_chat_map (Phase E). The UI loop pushes a fresh PanelData::SubagentList on each lifecycle event; render_viewport paints it in the gutter.

Requires PanelData / PanelMode extension: today PanelData carries todos + modified files for the RIGHT panel. Either extend with a Left field or add a separate LeftPanelData + render path. Coordinate with the existing avatar — should sit ABOVE the avatar, leaving the bottom 3 rows for it.

title: UI: subagent multi-chat-window (Ctrl-N/P, /tasks picker) — port from maki
description:
Each subagent task gets its own chat window; navigate via Ctrl-N (next) / Ctrl-P (previous) and /tasks slash command opens a picker. Maki: maki-ui/src/app/mod.rs:317-322 (open_tasks), :341-352 (Ctrl-N/P), components/keybindings.rs:135-136 (NEXT_CHAT/PREV_CHAT), components/command.rs:22-23 (/tasks).

Bigger refactor: dirge's renderer holds ONE buffer. Need to model Vec<Chat> with active_chat index, swap renderer buffer on switch. Conflict: dirge's Ctrl-X currently drops queued interjections — would need a different binding for next-task, or rebind interjection-drop. Coordinate with user before starting.
notes:
## Design (post-scope-decision: user picked Heavier refactor)

Five phases, each independently committable so we can ship partial
progress if needed.

### Phase A: Multi-buffer in Renderer (~200-300 lines)

Per-chat state extracted into a `ChatBuffer` struct:
- buffer: Vec<LineEntry>
- partial: CompactString + partial_color
- scroll_offset, lines, col
- selection_active, selection_start, selection_end

Global state stays on Renderer:
- input_rows, monochrome
- panel_mode, panel_data
- avatar_state, avatar_tick

Renderer gains:
- chats: Vec<ChatBuffer>
- active_chat: usize
- add_chat(name) -> ChatId
- switch_chat(id)
- chat_names()

All existing public methods (write_line, write, replace_from,
render_viewport, scroll_*, selection_*) route to chats[active_chat].

Backward compat: Renderer::new() creates one default chat (active=0).
Single-chat usage = existing behavior.

### Phase B: Keybinds + /tasks (~80 lines)

- Ctrl-N: next chat
- Ctrl-P: prev chat
- Ctrl-X: open /tasks picker (rebind interjection-drop to Alt+X
  per ov2 plan)
- `/tasks` slash command: same picker

Picker shows chat names; selection switches active.

### Phase C: Per-chat UI state (~150 lines)

ui/mod.rs has 10+ state variables that are currently per-session:
- response_buf, response_start_line
- reasoning_buf, reasoning_start_line
- last_tool_name, last_tool_call_id
- tool_chamber_open, agent_line_started
- was_reasoning, tool_calls_buf

Move to a `ChatUiState` struct, one per chat. The UI loop reads
state from `chat_states[active_chat]` on each event.

### Phase D: task tool refactor (~200 lines)

Currently `task` tool calls `model.btw_query(prompt)` — one-shot,
no tools. Refactor to:
- Spawn a sub-runner using same provider but limited tool set
  (read-only? or same as parent? — needs decision)
- Subagent emits AgentEvents to its own mpsc channel
- task tool returns the chat_id; UI routes events from the channel
  to chats[chat_id]

### Phase E: Event router (~80 lines)

UI loop selects across multiple agent_rx channels (one per active
subagent). Each event lands in chat_states[event.chat_id] and
renders via chats[event.chat_id].

## Open question for Phase D

Should subagent inherit parent's tool set, or be restricted to
read-only? Maki gives subagents full tools by default. Dirge's
permission system already gates per-tool, so safety isn't broken
either way. Inheriting is simpler.

title: CLI: --output-format stream-json headless mode (port from maki)
description:
dirge has --print boolean but no output format selector. Port from maki:
- maki/src/main.rs:32-58 (Cli with --print + --output-format)
- maki/src/print.rs:44-49 (OutputFormat enum: Text | Json | StreamJson)
- maki/src/print.rs:51-110 (PrintResult / InitEvent / AssistantEvent / UserEvent structs — Claude-Code-compatible JSON schema)
- maki/src/print.rs:126-388 (headless run loop with NDJSON event serialization at lines 280, 285, 304, 325, 334)

Adapt: dirge's run_print already exists at src/provider.rs:572. Extend it with format dispatch. For stream-json: serde-serialize each AgentEvent (or a JSON-shaped envelope) and println! one object per line.

title: Perm review F3: space-star pattern optional-arg semantics
description:
LOW-polish gap found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Opencode's wildcard matcher (util/wildcard.ts:13-15) rewrites trailing ' *' as '( .*)?' — making the trailing args optional. So 'ls *' matches BOTH 'ls' and 'ls -la'. Dirge's pattern.rs glob_to_regex requires the args (' *' becomes ' .*' which needs at least the space).

Concrete UX friction: a user accepts 'ls *' from a session allowlist after the agent runs 'ls -la', then the agent runs bare 'ls' — re-prompted because the saved pattern doesn't match. Port the optional-suffix rewrite to src/permission/pattern.rs's glob_to_regex.

title: B3-9: edit tool fuzzy-match cascade
description: edit.rs:155-160 returns 'old_text not found' on whitespace/indent/CRLF drift. LLMs frequently hit this. opencode edit.ts:222-432 has simple → lineTrimmed → whitespace-normalized → indentation-flexible → levenshtein. pi edit-diff.ts:91-132 has fuzzyFindText. Port the cascade.

title: B3-10: LSP pull-diagnostic fallback for lazy servers
description: lsp/client.rs:261-289 wait_for_push is push-only. clojure-lsp/jdtls/clangd may not push on demand. 10s timeout = 'clean' diagnostic block reported when errors exist. opencode lsp/client.ts:540-582 races push-wait against requestDocumentDiagnostics. Add pull fallback.

title: B3-8: soft-wrap + mouse selection display-width awareness
description: renderer.rs:1457-1496 wrap_input measures chars().count() instead of UnicodeWidthChar. Cursor mis-lands on CJK/emoji wrapped lines. Mouse selection columns map 1:1 char-to-display rather than width-aware. Pi interactive-mode.ts uses string-width per cell.

title: B3-7: Python find_callees captures method calls
description: semantic/adapters/python.rs:280 only matches (call function:(identifier)); misses obj.method(). Go/Java/C++ all capture selector_expression equivalent. Python users get hollow callee lists.

title: B3-5: doom-loop + allowlist reset on /cd
description: permission/checker.rs:568-571 only updates cwd. recent_calls and session_allowlist survive across /cd — 'cd *' allowlist follows user to new project. Clear both on set_working_dir.

title: B3-6: bash background & in fallback splitter + AST recurse
description: bash.rs:401-473 fallback splitter (no semantic-bash feature) doesn't split on bare &. Also semantic-bash should recurse so background commands get checked individually.

title: B3-4: /loop max-iterations default cap
description: ui/slash.rs:778 passes None for max_iterations; LoopState::should_stop returns false forever. Runaway API spend until user notices. Default cap 20; accept '/loop --max N <prompt>'.

title: B3-3: provider env-var fallbacks beyond GLM/ZHIPU
description: provider.rs:210-219 only chains ZHIPU. Add ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN as fallback for Anthropic, GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY for Gemini. Pi handles both.

title: B3-2: HTTP 500 retry + robust status patterns
description: recovery.rs:261-269 only retries 502/503/504. Add 500 + any 5xx. Patterns require literal ' NNN ' spaces; accept 'status: NNN', 'status=NNN', 'NNN ' too. opencode retries all status >= 500.

title: B3-1: memory tool atomic writes
description: extras/memory.rs:118 uses std::fs::write (truncate-in-place). Switch to crate::fs_atomic::atomic_write to match write.rs/edit.rs/apply_patch.rs pattern. Crash mid-write currently leaves truncated .md.

title: Batch2-3: session save lockfile to avoid two-instance data loss
description: session/storage.rs:55-74 uses atomic-rename per save but no advisory lock or version check. Two dirge instances on the same session id: last write wins, the loser's in-memory state silently overwritten. Add per-session-file fs2/fd-lock advisory lock OR version-stamp + reload-conflict warning. opencode uses lockfile; pi serializes through a process-local session manager.

title: Batch2-2: AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md ancestor walk depth cap
description: context/mod.rs:90-103 walks parent chain to /, reading both AGENTS.md AND CLAUDE.md at every level. Deep project under /Users/foo/work/x/y/z/... = 6-10 stat+open calls per startup plus unintended files under /Users/yogthos / /Users. opencode caps at git root + /Users/yogthos. Cap walk at: (a) git root if found, (b) max 8 levels otherwise. README claim 'ancestors + global fallback' preserved.

title: Batch2-1: pre-emptive compaction guard before user prompt dispatch
description: Auto-compact only fires on AgentEvent::ContextOverflow (ui/mod.rs:2543-2545 in Done arm). A new user prompt is sent against a near-full window → request fires → ContextOverflow → reactive compact + respawn. Costs an extra round-trip + provider error every slow recovery. opencode/pi compact BEFORE dispatch when needs_compaction() returns true. Add pre-flight check on user-prompt submit.

title: H-R1: try_lock at loop-top PM acquisitions to avoid UI freeze during plugin tools
description: ui/mod.rs event-loop top does std::sync::Mutex::lock() multiple times per iteration (list_shortcuts, drain_notifications, drain_entries, drain_tree_ops). During a plugin tool execution inside spawn_blocking holding the same mutex, these acquisitions block the runtime worker thread — UI freezes. Switch all four to try_lock; on contention, skip the refresh this iteration. drain_* semantics tolerate the one-tick delay; list_shortcuts gets refreshed on the next idle tick after the tool returns.

title: H3: expose prepareArguments on harness/register-tool
description:
LoopTool::prepare_arguments default no-op; JanetLoopTool doesn't override. Plugin authors wanting to normalize args before schema validation (pi's prepareEditArguments is the canonical example) have no surface.

Pi: prepareArguments?: (args: unknown) => Static<TParams> on ToolDefinition (types.ts:443); pi forwards through the wrapper.

Fix: optional prepare-arguments field on harness/register-tool (Janet fn name). Host calls it before parsing args into the loop's typed shape. Mutate result back into Value and feed to handler.

title: H4: dedup plugin-tool names + emit diagnostic on shortcut/renderer collisions
description:
harness/register-tool appends to harness-tools-list unconditionally. Two plugins (or one plugin reload pattern) registering the same name produce two entries; both are wrapped as JanetLoopTool and the agent loop has two same-name tools with undefined dispatch order.

Pi: extension.tools is Map<name, RegisteredTool> (loader.ts:219) — second set replaces first. Across extensions runner.ts:378 uses first-extension-wins.

Fix: dedup in list_plugin_tools (last-load-wins per name, matching the Map semantics) with tracing::warn on duplicate. Same for list_shortcuts; pi emits a diagnostic per duplicate (runner.ts:451). list_message_renderers already first-match-wins via .find().

title: H5: surface plugin shortcut handler errors instead of swallowing
description:
UI shortcut dispatch goes through invoke_command which catches Janet errors to Ok(None). UI only displays on Ok(Some(msg)). A handler raising an exception is indistinguishable from one returning nil — no chat notification, no tracing::warn, plugin authors get zero feedback.

Pi parallel: Promise.resolve(...).catch(err => this.showError(...)) (interactive-mode.ts:1666).

Fix: use the same DIRGE_HOOK_ERR catch wrapper that dispatch() uses (mod.rs:1956) — tracing::warn + harness/push-hook-err notification. Either inline in invoke_command (with backward-compat), or add a new invoke_command_traced variant.

title: 9b: align harness/register-command + harness/register-provider to ExtensionApi
description: The existing harness/register-command and harness/register-provider were added ad-hoc. Refactor them to route through ExtensionApi (added in 9a) so all register-* APIs share one shape. Keep backwards compatibility for any in-tree plugins by leaving the old call sites as thin wrappers. Acceptance: provider/command registration goes through ExtensionApi; no behavior change for users.
acceptance_criteria: Provider + command registration both route through ExtensionApi. Existing tests still pass. No new Janet API breakage.
notes: On closer review, the wire-format difference between register-command (name|handler), register-provider (name|type|base|env), and register-tool (tab-escape) is cosmetic. Existing parsers are stable, no user surfaces are affected, and the Rust-side return types are already shaped per their use site. Aligning them would be churn for marginal gain. Deferring in favor of 9c (registerShortcut) which delivers new capability.

title: 9a: ExtensionApi surface + harness/register-tool
description: Build foundation + first feature together. Add an ExtensionApi struct in src/plugin/extension.rs that exposes register_tool/register_command/register_provider/register_hook in a uniform shape. Wire it into PluginManager. Add harness/register-tool helper in Janet that lets plugins register a LoopTool whose execute() callback runs a named Janet function. The plugin-tool list must be discoverable by build_loop_tools() so the agent loop picks them up alongside built-ins. Acceptance: a Janet plugin can declare a tool with name+description+parameters+handler, and the LLM can call it. Tool parameters use JSON Schema (matching pi's TypeBox shape semantically).
acceptance_criteria: Janet plugin can call (harness/register-tool ...) with name, description, parameters, handler-fn. Tool appears in build_loop_tools(). Test invokes the registered tool via run_agent_loop and checks the Janet handler ran with correct args.

title: Phase 9 epic: pi-style extension API for plugins
description: Refactor dirge's plugin architecture to mirror pi's extension API. Goal: a unified ExtensionApi surface (in Rust + Janet harness) that lets plugins register tools, commands, providers, shortcuts, and message renderers using one consistent shape. Current state: dirge has harness/register-command, harness/register-provider, harness/register-hook (string-based slot-setters). Missing: registerTool (biggest gap), registerShortcut, registerMessageRenderer. Sub-phases tracked separately.

title: H10/H17/M7b/M13 followup — reviewer flagged incomplete
description: Reviewer caught 4 partial/missing fixes after R-batch: H10 needed agent interject too; H17 needed AgentEvent::ContextOverflow + auto-respawn; M7b grep had no per-line cap; M13 still .write() on the outer RwLock.
close_reason: Addressed in this commit. H10 also fires interject; H17 emits AgentEvent::ContextOverflow → UI auto-compacts + respawns; grep adds 4 KiB per-line cap with UTF-8-safe truncation marker; SymbolIndex now uses Mutex<FileCache> so all 5 semantic tools hold .read() on the outer RwLock.
